communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: 321D-IR-A7788
communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the FBI memos referenced in this New York Times story: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/25/us/politics/trump-epstein-files.html Specifically, I seek a copy of any and all "F.B.I. memos summarizing interviews the bureau did in connection to claims made in 2019 by a woman who came forward after Mr. Epstein’s arrest to say she had been sexually assaulted by both Mr. Trump and Mr. Epstein decades earlier, when she was a minor." The FBI wrote four such memos, according to the New York Times report, but three of them have not been released publicly. We seek to obtain those three memos. For more information about this case, see the attached document. 

I am a reporter for The Nation magazine and other news outlets, and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied, I am willing to pay $25 for these records.

I seek expedited processing for this request. The information I seek could shed light on a matter of urgent and compelling public interest -- namely, the potential involvement of federal government personnel in the alleged sex trafficking operation organized by the late Jeffrey Epstein. This information is at the center of public discourse at this moment, and the Epstein case is a matter that involves the White House, Congress and numerous judicial bodies. It is imperative that the public obtain information on this matter in a timely matter, particularly given the corrosive effects the Epstein case has had on trust in government. For the reasons above, I ask that my request be given expedited processing because there is an urgency to inform the public about actual or alleged federal government activity, and I am a person primarily engaged in disseminating information. I intend to use the requested records to inform the public about actual government activity. I certify the foregoing to be true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

title: Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into forensic scientist Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/10/01/science/edward-t-blake-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Right to Know Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Right to Know Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e
See attached.  There is no data available for any of the requests prior to August of 2023 that I could locate.

title: Texas Tech guidance on gender discussions

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Texas Tech guidance on gender discussions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any COMMUNICATIONS (defined below) to or from Chancellor Tedd L. Mitchell or any president or provost of any campus within the Texas Tech University System concerning any of: (a) Chancellor Tedd L. Mitchell's September 25, 2025 memorandum entitled "Message to University Presidents Regarding Compliance" (available at https://www.texastech.edu/downloads/9-25-25-memo-to-university-presidents-regarding-compliance.pdf); (b) House Bill 229 (c) Governor Abbott's January 30, 2025 letter concerning sex and gender (available at https://gov.texas.gov/uploads/files/press/State_Agency_Heads_01.30.25.pdf); (cd) President Trump’s January 20, 2025, Executive Order, Defending Women from Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government; or (e) teaching, curriculum, classes, or academics concerning sex or gender. 

2. Any COMMUNICATIONS (defined below) to or from Chancellor Tedd L. Mitchell or any president or provost of any campus within the Texas Tech University System mentioning any of the following terms: "gender", "transgender", "trans", "executive order", "Abbott", "governor", "Bill 229", "HB 229", "H.B. 229", "First Amendment", "academic freedom". 

3. Any COMMUNICATIONS (defined below) between any person listed in (a), on the one hand, and any person listed in (b), on the other hand:
(a) Cody Campbell; Dustin R. Womble; Tedd L. Mitchell; Sydney Aalbers; Martha Brown; Jennifer Brown; Krista Butts; Emily Cormier; Worth Farabee; Cole Johnson; Debbie Martinez; John Opperman; Bogan Pollock; Robert L. Duncan;
(b) Gov. Greg Abbott, Robert Black, Tabatha Vasquez, or Michael Hull.

These requests are limited to records generated on or after September 10, 2025.

"COMMUNICATIONS" means and includes emails, text messages, direct messages sent via social media (but not including comments or messages posted for public view), letters, memoranda, calendar entries, chats, or any other form of one-to-one or one-to-many communication.

Please note that this request seeks public records wherever they may be found, including on devices or networks owned or operated by private parties. For example, if an employee uses a personal cell phone or social media account to conduct public business, records created, maintained, or held on the phone or account are public records subject to this request.

Please note that this request does not seek a search of faculty or student email accounts or records. These requests should in no way be construed to include a review or search of email accounts, websites, or other forms of data or document retention which are controlled by students, alumni, or faculty members, nor by governmental or advisory bodies controlled by the same, except insofar as any such individuals also serve in an administrative capacity and the records were created or are held by the individual in that capacity. Any search should be limited to documents held by the administration and/or its staff members, including records created or maintained by persons acting in the capacity of administrators or staff members.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: FW: Texas Public Information Act Request: Texas Tech guidance on gender discussions
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Steinbaugh,

Texas Tech University System (TTUS) is in receipt of your Texas Public Information Act request below.  However, TTUS seeks to discuss with you how the scope of your request might be narrowed and/or clarified pursuant to Texas Government Code § 552.222(b).  TTUS requests that you narrow or clarify the scope of your request as follows:


  1.  We noticed that for part 3 of your request, you do not provide any specific terms.  Thus, it is unclear if you are seeking any/all communications among those individuals or if you are seeking any communications with the same terms provided within part 2 of your request.  If you could clarify whether the specific terms apply, then we will be able to process this request. Please note, if you are seeking any/all communications, the search may be prohibitively expensive as some of the people you have named are part of our government relations teams and they often interact with state administration as part of their duties.


  1.  With regard to the rest of your request, please note, policy discussions and draft policies are generally withheld under the "deliberative process privilege;" thus, we may send this information to the Attorney General for review prior to release.

Please simply respond to this request and let us know if you are willing to narrow/clarify the scope of your request in the manner suggested or in some other manner.  Please note that we may be required to send some information to the Texas Attorney General for review prior to release.  That process could take 2-3 months.  TTUS is required to inform you that if you do not respond to this request for narrowing/clarification within 61 days from the date of this request, your request will be considered withdrawn.

Candace Norrod, CP
NALA Certified Paralegal
[GC_fl2C.jpg]
P.O. Box 42021, Lubbock, Texas 79409-2021
T 806.742-2155
candace.norrod@ttu.edu<mailto:candace.norrod@ttu.edu>

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: Texas Tech guidance on gender discussions
communications.communication:
Hello --

With respect to Request No. 3, I am not limiting it by specific terms. However, this request can be limited to communications between September 10, 2025 and October 1, 2025.

With respect to the deliberative process privilege, I understand that the information may be sent to the Attorney General for review. I would appreciate that records be produced on a rolling basis if they become available while the review process is underway. 

Thank you,

Adam Steinbaugh

title: California Public Records Act. Request: CPRA: Flock Safety

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: California Public Records Act. Request: CPRA: Flock Safety
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,

I am requesting records related to the installation, approval, and precise locations/addresses of ALL automatic license plate readers (ALPR) from Flock Safety/Flock Group, Inc.

Emails between city personnel, SPPD, and Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc. employees/domains (e.g., @flocksafety.com).
Including, but not limited to:
Akeia Dixon - installs@flocksafety.com
Dave@flocksafety.com
garrett@flocksafety.com
Do not include promo emails or emails from hello@flocksafety.com

All communications related to installations.
Keyword search:
"installations"
"install"
"technicians"
"public works"
"rebates"

Copies of attachments:
All documentation and records of installations that include their dates, locations, addresses, approvals by the city for installations, and all contracts related to the installations.
All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, vendor agreements, letters of intent, Statement of Work documents, Scope of Work (SOW) documents, policy approvals, and all other documents with Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc.

Policies & Procedures. The Policies for Flock Safety use, data retention, sharing, and access.
Agreements allowing Flock Safety or  Flock Group, Inc. to share data with federal or any other agencies.
Names and positions of all individuals in the Santa Paula Police Department and/or City of Santa Paula that have the ability to access and search for any data collected by the Flock Safety ALPR's.

Limit the above searches from the period of 2018, to day this request is processed.

Audits:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 21, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 21, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anthony Jiminez

Dear Anthony, 
  
 Thank you for contacting the City of Santa Paula, where we are committed to helping you. 
  
 This letter shall serve as an initial response notifying you that the City has received and is reviewing your request for public records, pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.). 
  
 The City is in the process of evaluating the records request you submitted in an effort to make any relevant and disclose-able documents promptly available to you. (See Government Code Section 6253(b).) Please note that whether and when any documents will be produced subject to your request depends on the payment of any applicable fees, and the time that it takes the City to search for, collect and redact exempt information from the records requested, if necessary. 
  
 In the meantime, if you have any questions or concerns regarding the processing of this request, please contact me directly. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Julie Latshaw
City Clerk 
  
 805-933-4208

communications.subject: Partial Production of Records for PRA #
communications.communication:
Dear Anthony, 
  
 This correspondence is in response to your Public Records Act Request (“PRA”) received by the City of Santa Paula (“City"). The City is providing responsive records for your review 
  
 We estimate that an update of the disclosable responsive records will be available to you beginning on 4/24/2026. Please note that we are seeking to produce all responsive records as promptly as possible on a rolling production basis. If we can make the records available prior to the production date indicated above, we will do so. Likewise, if additional time will be needed to complete the review and redaction of responsive records, we will so notify you in writing. 
  
 Sincerely, 
  
 Julie Latshaw, CMC 
  
 City Clerk

communications.subject: 14 Day Extension
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Jiminez, 
  
 Thank you for your email correspondence dated 4/6/2026, requesting a public document. The City will attempt to fully comply with your request for documents. Accordingly, you may inspect (and copy) all documents the City has pertaining to your request except those that are exempt from disclosure under the Public Records Act. 
  
 Please note that the Public Records Act (“PRA”) requires the City to disclose “writings” that it keeps in the ordinary course of business (Gov’t. Code § 7920.510, et seq.). It does not, however, require the City to generate new records in response to a citizen's request for documents (Rosenthal v. Hansen (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 754). Nor does the PRA require the City to comply with requests for documents that are unduly burdensome (State Bd. of Equalization v. Super. Ct. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1177.) 
  
 Moreover, the PRA exempts certain types of documents from disclosure (see generally Gov’t. Code §§ 7927.500, et seq., 7922.000). Accordingly, the City respectfully declines to release the following types of documents: 
  
 Government Code § 7927.705 specifically exempts records that are protected under state and federal law. The documents available to you are those not protected by attorney-client, or other, privilege (see Gov’t. Code § 7930,110); 
 Similarly, records prepared in anticipation of litigation to which the City is a party are not being disclosed (Gov’t. Code § 7927.200); 
 Records that would result in invading the privacy of an individual or corporation will either be redacted or not disclosed (Gov’t. Code §§ 1798.3, 7927.700, 7922.0;


 
 We anticipate that it will take additional time to search for, collect, and review the volume of information you requested. According to Government Code § 7922.535, the City will require an additional fourteen (14) days to identify and further all of the records pertaining to your public records request. 
  
 Julie Latshaw, CMC
City Clerk
City of Santa Paula

communications.subject: Your Town of Weymouth public records request #26-2097 has been closed.
communications.communication:
Town of Weymouth ************************************************************************ Record request #26-2097 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: All responsive records have been released. It is possible some information contained in responsive record(s) is exempt from the definition of a public record. If so, some or all of your response MAY have been redacted or completely withheld for the following reason(s):  Medical Files or Information G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(c) – Exemption (c) [Medical Files or Information Exemption] – Medical information constitutes intimate details of a highly personal nature where the risk of an invasion of privacy outweighs the benefits of public disclosure. As a general rule, such information related to an identifiable individual will always be of a sufficiently personal nature to warrant exemption, as there is strong public policy in Massachusetts favoring confidentiality of medical data about a person’s body. See SPR 17/386, p.2; A Guide to Massachusetts Public Records Law, Mass. Sec. of State, March 2020, p. 17. This exemption will also apply to any information concerning injuries, medical condition, or mental illness. See also G.L. 111, §70E [Patient’s Rights to Confidentiality]. General Privacy Exemption G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(c) – Exemption (c) [Privacy Exemption] – Applies to any materials or data relating to a specifically named individual, the disclosure of which may constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, where the public interest in obtaining the requested information does not substantially outweigh the seriousness of any invasion of privacy. Examples include marital status, legitimacy of children, identity of fathers of children, medical condition, welfare payments, alcohol consumption, family fights, and reputation. See Boston Globe Media Partners, LLC v. Dep’t of Pub. Health, 482 Mass. 427, 443 n.17 (2019). Reports, Communications, and Records Related to Calls and Responses of Domestic Violence, Rape, Sexual Assault, and Human Trafficking Reports G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(a) – Exemption (a) – Statutory Exemption [G.L. c. 41, §§97D and 98F] – All reports of rape and sexual assault or attempts to commit such offenses, all reports of abuse perpetrated by family or household members, as defined in section 1 of chapter 209A, and all communications between police officers and victims of such offenses or abuse “shall not be public reports and shall be maintained by the police departments in a manner that shall assure their confidentiality.” G.L. c. 41, §97D. Police log entries concerning responses to and arrests for same shall also not be disclosed. G.L. c. 41, §98F. G.L. c. 265, §24 also prohibits release of any record containing the name of a victim in an arrest, investigation, or complaint of certain sexual assault offenses and human trafficking. Finally, reports are likely also exempted from disclosure via G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(c) – Exemption (c) [Privacy Exemption], as the risk of unwarranted invasion of personal privacy outweighs the public interest in the requested information regarding documentation of marital status, sexual assault, and family fights. See Boston Globe Media Partners, LLC v. Dep’t of Pub. Health, 482 Mass. 427, 443 n.17 (2019). Certain 209A Abuse Prevention/258E Harassment Prevention Order Information G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(a) – Exemption (a) – Statutory Exemption [G.L. c. 209A, §8 and c. 258E, §10] – A plaintiff’s home and work address, phone number, and workplace name are exempt from disclosure. G.L. c. 41, §98F also precludes disclosure of certain records in the event of an arrest for violation of an order issued pursuant to c. 209A. In any event, such reports are likely also exempted from disclosure via G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(c) – Exemption (c) [Privacy Exemption] as it concerns family disputes. We are required to inform requesters they may appeal a decision to redact or withhold responsive records to the Supervisor of Public Records, pursuant to 950 CMR 32.08(1)(d). By law, the Supervisor is required to respond within 10 business days of receipt of an appeal. A requester may also seek judicial review of an unfavorable response by commencing a civil action in the superior court, under G.L. c. 66, §10A(c). More information can be located on the website of the Secretary of the Commonwealth at https://www.sec.state.ma.us/divisions/public-records/public-records-law/appealing-a-denial.htm. This request is now closed. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Town of Weymouth.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Alhambra Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Alhambra Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Iowa Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Iowa Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: Washington DOC Adult Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Washington DOC Adult Room and Board + Medical Copay Fee Information
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of inmate fees and debt:

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical copays and room & board costs or fees for which inmates are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding total fees collected from inmates for medical copays and room and board fees from 2020-2024. Please include the number of individuals charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed this past calendar year. Please also include the total amount of room and board fees collected through the work-release program.

If this request is overly burdensome or requires a substantial amount of labor, please provide the materials for the past calendar year.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 11/19/2025 and given the reference number P057386-111925 for tracking purposes. 
This message is an automated confirmation of your request, and does not constitute an official Department acknowledgement under RCW 42.56.520. Agency staff will provide a separate correspondence formally acknowledging this request within 5 business days.

  * All current policies and procedures regarding medical copays and room & board costs or fees for which inmates are or may become liable. title: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Find responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education. Proposals were due on 6/7/2023.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Toni Thompson

communications.subject: PRR 25-034 Florida Sunshine Law Request: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education
communications.communication:
Please accept this as the Department’s acknowledgment of your request for information.

Your request has been forwarded to Department staff for review and response. The Department will respond in a reasonable time based on the effort required to locate, review, and redact the requested information. Please be advised, requests are addressed in the order they are received.

The assigned number for this request is PRR 25-034.

I have attached the Department’s Public Records Request Guide. Thank you and have a great day.

Sincerely,

Public Records Unit
Florida Department of Education
325 West Gaines Street, Suite 1544
Tallahassee, FL 32399-0400
Phone: 850-245-9759
Email: PRR@fldoe.org<mailto:PRR@fldoe.org>

Good afternoon,

Thank you for contacting the Department of Education. We have completed the review of this request and all responsive documents will be provided to you by way of a ShareFile in a separate email (the documents are too large to send via email). These materials constitute all of the documents held by the Department of Education that relate to your public records request.

You should receive the email with access to the ShareFile in one to two hours. Please note, the file will be deleted in 7 days.

This public record request is now complete, and your file will be closed. Thank you and have a great day.

Sincerely,


Public Records Unit

Florida Department of Education

325 West Gaines Street, Suite 1544

Tallahassee, FL 32399-0400

Phone: 850-245-9759

Email: PRR@fldoe.org<mailto:PRR@fldoe.org>

title: Ohio Public Records Request – Body-Worn Camera - Arrest of Paige Cox (Aug 23, 2022)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Ohio Public Records Request – Body-Worn Camera - Arrest of Paige Cox (Aug 23, 2022)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the arrest of Paige Cox (born 06/08/1994) on August 23, 2022, by the Cincinnati Police Department.

The arrest followed a single-vehicle crash. Ms. Cox refused a chemical test after being read Ohio's ALS form, and was cited under R.C. 4511.19. The arrest occurred in Hamilton County, Ohio.

The involved officers were identified by the following badge numbers:

• Badge P895 – Arresting Officer

title: CI Staff Record Study

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CI Staff Record Study
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the 29 November 1976 memorandum to Chief Information Management Staff, "CI Staff Record Study" from David H. Blee (Chief, Counterintelligence Staft) as well as records set a, b, d, e, f, g, h, and i described on pages two and three of the memo.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1674627-000
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to appeal the determination of FOIA request 1674627-000, in which I requested the budget of the FBI's Office of Public Affairs for 2022.

In your response, you sent me a link to a speech delivered in 2021 by the former FBI director before the Senate Appropriations Committee. While this speech does contain certain information about the FBI budget, it does not mention the Office of Public Affairs specifically and is therefore not responsive to my request.

I therefore believe that my request has not been fulfilled in accordance with FOIA. I reiterate my request for the 2022 annual budget report for the FBI's Office of Public Affairs.

Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

title: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents mentioning or relating to Anonymous (the hacker movement/collective, see below) or hacktivism (defined as hacking as a form of protest and/or activism) generated between 1 January 2009 and 1 January 2019, including but not limited to:

* Internal reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to Anonymous or hacktivism
* Reports from the appropriate IT (Information Technology) or computer offices regarding possible cyber attacks by Anonymous or other hacktivists
* Reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to or mentioning Anonymous or hacktivism sent to or received from any federal law enforcement or intelligence agencies or any fusion center
* Incident reports investigating possible hacking activity falling within the office's jurisdiction and allegedly carried out by members of Anonymous or other hacktivist groups
* Materials generated as a result of any suspected or actual compromise, breach or "dox" (revealing of personal or private information) by Anonymous or other hacktivists

Anonymous has been described by government agencies as a non-hierarchical hacktivist collective, Anonymous uses hacking (and arguably cracking) techniques to register political protest in campaigns known as “#ops.” Best known for their distributed denial of services (DDoS) attacks, past activities have included attacks against the Church of Scientology; Visa, Paypal, and others who withdrew their services from WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange after that group began releasing war documents; #OpTunisia and others purporting to support the Arab Spring; and a campaign that brought down the website of the Westboro Baptist Church. #Ops are usually marked with the release of a video of a reader in a Guy Fawkes mask using a computer generated voice. See attached for additional background information.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best
An interim response, stating the request is being processed.
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Victor Lappohn
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: November 24, 1940
Dates of Service: Approx 1960 to 1975

Request follwing documents:
1) Bronze Star Medal award citations
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) cumulative Navy Occupational training history NAVPERS 601-4 and 1070/604,
4)  military education and courses
5) Navy commendation medal award citation

Historical Documentation, Vietnam War history

first name: Victor
last name: Lappohn

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
Hello-

We are in receipt of your request.  We are working to compile the
information you request; you can expect a response within 10 business days.

Thanks,

Tim

-- 
Timothy Ball
Business Administrator
SAU88 (Lebanon, NH)

-- 
_*The Right-To-Know Law* provides that most email communications, to or 
from Lebanon School District employees regarding the business of the School 
District, are government records available to the public upon request. 
Therefore, this email communication may be subject to public disclosure. 
This email is intended solely for the person or entity to which it is 
addressed and may contain confidential and/or privileged information. Any 
review, dissemination, copying, printing, or other use of this email by 
persons or entities other than the addressee is strictly prohibited. If you 
receive this email in error, please notify the sender immediately and 
delete the material from any computer.
_

title: Navy support for "Flight Ready" (2024), short film for Apple Vision Pro

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for "Flight Ready" (2024), short film for Apple Vision Pro
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of the 2024 short film "Flight Ready," produced for the Apple Vision Pro. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Flight Ready," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Flight Ready" appeared (under the title "Flightdeck") in a list for Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request. 

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Police Department - Beverly, MA)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Police Department - Beverly, MA)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1  - May 10, 2011

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1  - May 10, 2011
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents -- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1, 2011 and May 10, 2011. (When I made this request to ONDCP, the agency said it did not have any of the records I was seeking, and, because of the time period in question -- 2011 -- suggested I send the request to NARA, instead.)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Philip Eil

received by this office on April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the
National Archives and Records Administration (NARA). Your request was
assigned FOIA Tracking Number *RD 67366*.

We are continuing to work on logging in FOIA requests in the order they are
received. Once your request reaches the front of the initial response
queue, a staff member will search for the record(s) and provide you with a

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

Unfortunately, the COVID-19 pandemic has created many unprecedented
challenges in our work. The majority of the records that our customers
request require our staff to be on-site to search for and review microfilm,
microfiche, or original textual records. Even as we expand our reopening,
operations may be temporarily curtailed due to limited staffing and/or
deteriorating local health conditions.

Due to limitations on staff in our facility over the course of the
pandemic, the backlog of FOIA requests has significantly expanded, and we
are continuing to work on logging in and providing initial responses to the
FOIA requests we received over the past 26 months.

When we receive a request, we inform you in writing that we received your
request and provide you with a FOIA tracking number. Then, your request is
placed in our initial response queue waiting for the next available staff
member to conduct a search. Once the search is complete, you will be
contacted directly and informed of the results of the search and provided
an estimated completion date for the line-by-line review of the file as
requested under FOIA. Our small staff has and will continue to work
diligently to process each request as quickly as possible.

I want to assure you that we are aware of your request, and at this time,
your request is still in our initial search queue waiting for a search to
be conducted. Due to the size of the backlog at this time, we are not able
to provide you with an estimated timeframe of when someone will reach your
request and start the search process.

We appreciate your continued patience.

Sincerely,

Ashney Randle
Government Information Specialist
Special Access & FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated
April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the National Archives and
Records Administration (NARA). You requested access to “any records --
itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents --
reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil
Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1, 2011
and May 10, 2011.” Your request was received in this office on April 20,
2022 and assigned reference number RD 67366.

We have taken legal custody of the emails of the Federal components of the
Executive Office of the President from the Obama administration and were
able to search them for records that could be responsive to your request.

We have attached a spreadsheet that details the different search parameters
we used as well as the resulting number of potentially responsive documents
for each search.

Column A provides the basic search parameters that were used. We searched
for 1.) emails sent from Kerlikowske 2.) emails sent to Kerlikowske 3.)
emails Kerlikowske was CC’d on 4.) emails Kerlikowske was BCC’d on.

Column B provides the number of search results using only the basic search
parameters from Column A.This column provides the overall number of emails
sent to/from Kerlikowske regarding any/all topics during the requested date
range. For example, block B2 indicates that Kerikowske sent 6,081 total
emails during the requested time frame.

Column C adds a filter to the search results of Column B. It filters out
some spam mail. The emails excluded include the following words: "newsletter"
"e-newsletter" "newspaper article"  "daily article" "news summary" "public
mail" "public opinion mail"  "clip" "clips"

Column D provides the number of search results using key words mentioned in
your request. The key words used were: "itinerary" "itineraries" "schedule"
"press release" "press releases" "travel" "appearance" "appearances" "event"

Column E adds a filter to the search results of Column D. It filters out
some spam mail. The emails excluded include the following words: "newsletter"
"e-newsletter" "newspaper article"  "daily article" "news summary" "public
mail" "public opinion mail"  "clip" "clips"

Please let us know which set of searches you would like us to consider
responsive to your search. While making your decision, please keep in mind
the amount of time it will take to process requests of varying sizes. If
your total request is for less than 141 responsive emails, it can be added
to our first tier processing queue, which has an estimated processing time
of 39 months. Requests for 141 - 600 emails will be added to our second
tier processing queue.  Reviewers are currently processing second tier
requests that were received in June 2014.  Requests for more than 600
emails will be added to our third tier processing queue and processed on an
incremental basis.

Are you interested in receiving correspondence regarding automatic replies
to emails, out of office responses, notifications of canceled meetings, and
notifications of accepting or declining invitations? These types of
materials are currently included in the search results. Please let us know
if you would like us to exclude these types of materials. This option might
help in narrowing the results. Additionally, if there are any other
keywords you are particularly interested in which might narrow the results
more, we would be happy to run another set of searches.

These records require screening for categories of information exempted from
disclosure under the terms of the FOIA prior to public release. In
particular, information may be redacted to protect the personal privacy of
living individuals.

Given the initial search results, your request has been assigned to our
third tier processing queue. This is a temporary assignment until we know
your preferred search parameters and the number of records we will need to
review. Please contact me directly to let me know how you would like us to
proceed.

You must notify this office of changes to your contact information. If
communication is returned as undeliverable and no return address is
provided, your case will be administratively closed. To change your contact
information or to track the status of your request, please email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or call 301-837-3190. Please include your FOIA
tracking number in all communications concerning this request.

If you are not satisfied with our action on this request and would like the
opportunity to discuss our response, you may contact our FOIA Public
Liaison for assistance:

Accessioned Executive Branch Records – Washington, DC Area

FOIA Requester Service Center: 301-837-3190

FOIA Public Liaison: Rebecca Calcagno

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5500

College Park, MD 20740-6001

dc.foia.liaison@nara.gov

301-837-3190


If you have other questions regarding your request, please contact me
directly.



Sincerely,


Brigitte Flynn

Archives Specialist

Special Access and FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park, MD

https://www.archives.gov/research/foia

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Hi, Brigitte 

Thank you for your email, and my apologies for the delayed response.

Given the scale of the records I initially asked about, I'm wondering if it would be possible to narrow the window of my inquiry to between April 17th and April 22nd, 2011.

Thus, my new request would be:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents -- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between April 17, 2011 and April 22, 2011.  

Please advise me if you would need any additional info from me before proceeding with the fulfillment of this updated request.

Thank you!

-Phil 

philipeil.com 
401 374 5868 
philip.edward.eil@gmail.com
@phileil 
facebook.com/phileilwriter

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Requester Philip Eil--


This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request
dated April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the National Archives
and Records Administration (NARA). You initially requested access to “any
records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents
-- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil
Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1,
2011 and May 10, 2011.” Your request was received in this office on April
20, 2022 and assigned reference number RD 67366.


On November 29, 2022, you requested to narrow the window of your request to
between April 17 and April 22, 2011.  This narrows the results to a total
of 37 emails.  I have attached a worksheet that details the search terms I
used to determine this number. Please note that there may be overlap
between the various search results lists.


These records require review for information protected from release under
the FOIA: https://www.archives.gov/foia/foia-guide#determine.

As narrowed, I can move your request from the third tier processing queue
to our faster first-tier processing queue. Taking into consideration our
existing backlog, we estimate it will take 39 months to complete processing.

Once we notify you that processing is complete, you may either visit our
College Park, MD facility to view the file at no cost, or order a
reproduction for a fee according to our fee schedule
<https://www.archives.gov/research/order/fees>.

You must notify this office of changes to your contact information. If
communication is returned as undeliverable and no return address is
provided, your case will be administratively closed. To change your contact
information or to track the status of your request, please email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or call 301-837-3190. Please include your FOIA
tracking number in all communications concerning this request.

If you are not satisfied with our action on this request and would like the
opportunity to discuss our response, you may contact our FOIA Public
Liaison for assistance:

Accessioned Executive Branch Records – Washington, DC Area

FOIA Requester Service Center: 301-837-3190

FOIA Public Liaison: Rebecca Calcagno

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5500

College Park, MD 20740-6001

dc.foia.liaison@nara.gov

301-837-3190

If you have other questions regarding your request, please contact me
directly. Ms. Flynn, with whom you have had prior contact on this request,
has transferred to another division.


This email is UNCLASSIFIED.


Dr. A. M. Weimer

Supervisory Archivist

Special Access and FOIA Program (RF)

(pronouns: they/them, she/her)

National Archives and Records Administration

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5510

College Park, MD 20740-6001

(301) 837-0604

We have received your request and you will receive a response within 20 working days. Please note that submitting duplicate requests will increase wait times.  

If you have any questions, you can call our office at 301-837-3190. You can also visit our web page at www.archives.gov/research/foia.  


Thank you.
